Issue: Utility pages are 200 + indexable + present in sitemap
Evidence: /login/; /my-account/; /bookmarks/; /my-follows/ are Indexable in coinlineup-internal_html.csv and IN_SITEMAP in sitemaps_urls_in_sitemap.csv
Impact: High index bloat, crawl waste, weakens content quality signals, exposes low-value UX pages to Google
Recommended Action: Set noindex,follow or block indexing at template level; remove from XML sitemaps; review internal links that surface these URLs

Issue: Sitemap includes low-value and non-content URLs
Impact: Sitemap stops acting as a clean canonical indexable set and sends mixed quality signals
Recommended Action: Restrict sitemap generation to canonical index-worthy pages and trust pages that are intentionally indexable

Issue: Currency URLs redirect to a broken destination
Evidence: response_codes_redirection_(3xx).csv shows multiple /currencies/ URLs 301 to /cmc-currency-details-advanced-design/; sitemaps_nonindexable_urls_in_sitemap.csv shows that target is 404
Impact: Users and crawlers hit dead-end redirect chains; equity from internal links is wasted
Recommended Action: Fix the currency template routing, map each currency to a valid canonical destination, and remove the broken target from all sitemaps and internal links

Evidence: Internal error split from exports: 559 internal 404 URLs, 537 internal 301 URLs, 132 internal 5xx URLs
Impact: Weakens crawl efficiency, creates poor UX, and can trap Google in stale URL patterns
Recommended Action: Export inlinks for top broken internal URLs, update source links, and clean legacy slugs plus stale widget/template outputs

Issue: Broken and unstable uploads are included in sitemap coverage
Evidence: sitemaps_nonindexable_urls_in_sitemap.csv contains 141 non-indexables in sitemap, including 130 /wp-content/uploads/ entries; internal 5xx sample is dominated by uploads returning 520/522/525
Impact: Image and media instability hurts rendering, trust, and sitemap quality
Recommended Action: Remove failing media URLs from sitemap generation, verify CDN or origin stability, and regenerate missing image assets

Issue: Uncategorized archive is indexable and heavily linked
Evidence: https://coinlineup.com/uncategorized/ is 200 + Indexable with 1,292 unique inlinks and is present in sitemap
Impact: Thin archive pages dilute topic focus and can absorb link equity from meaningful sections
Recommended Action: Reassign posts out of Uncategorized, 301 or noindex the archive, and remove it from menus/widgets/sitemaps

Issue: Sponsored archive pagination is fully indexable
Evidence: 32 /sponsored-articles/ URLs are indexable, including paginated archives page/2 through page/32
Impact: Creates a large low-value commercial archive layer that can compete with editorial sections
Recommended Action: Decide whether sponsored content should index at archive level; if not, noindex paginated archive pages and separate them clearly from editorial taxonomy

Evidence: 423 author URLs are noindex in directives_noindex.csv, but /author/digitalanand18gmail-com/page/314/ is Indexable and listed in canonicals_missing.csv
Impact: Signals template leakage and can create index pollution on deep pagination
Recommended Action: Apply consistent noindex/canonical rules to author pagination and fix the template edge case creating page/314

Issue: Metadata debt is concentrated in low-value archives and utility areas
Evidence: 1,426 missing meta descriptions, 2,239 titles over 60 chars, 65 duplicate titles, 44 duplicate meta descriptions; missing meta is dominated by tag archives (1,218 rows)
Impact: Weak CTR and large-scale template duplication reduce SERP quality
Recommended Action: Start with template-level fixes for tag archives, duplicate posts, and utility pages before editing article metadata manually

Issue: Duplicate post variants with -2 suffix are live
Evidence: page_titles_duplicate.csv shows 18 duplicate-title URLs ending in -2; meta_description_duplicate.csv shows 17 duplicate-meta URLs ending in -2
Impact: Near-duplicate pages split signals and create cannibalization risk
Recommended Action: Choose canonical winners, 301 duplicate variants, and tighten editorial publishing workflow to prevent duplicate slugs

Evidence: sitemaps_orphan_urls.csv lists /contacts/, /editorial-policy/, and /publish-editorial-standards-fact-checking-policy/ as orphan URLs
Impact: Trust and policy pages are harder for users and crawlers to discover
Recommended Action: Link trust pages from footer, about, and author areas so they are not sitemap-only destinations

Issue: A large share of 4xx/5xx findings come from third-party destinations
Evidence: Of 3,705 total 4xx URLs, 3,144 are external; of 234 total 5xx URLs, 102 are external
Impact: Not as urgent as internal defects, but still degrades reader experience and QC quality
Recommended Action: Audit outbound links on high-traffic posts and replace blocked or dead references where they matter editorially
